prep.summary: You and Jonathan work across two major threads — Project Genie (AI-driven Jira/acceptance criteria automation) and Architecture as Code (three-tier data model, semantic tooling, GitLab Pages documentation) — with both efforts now past the blueprint phase and pushing toward MVP and governance alignment. The 5/14 meeting left open questions around entering the GenAI Council queue for Genie's 'top cover,' completing the YAML-based taxonomy migration, and scoping the full 'Business Analyst Coworker' vision — while the broader AaC effort is waiting on stakeholder alignment with Osterkamp and McCarty to move out of greenfield mode.
tasks: Confirm whether Genie has been formally submitted to the GenAI Council queue and what the current status is.
Clarify the state of the YAML/taxonomy migration — specifically whether the HTML-from-YAML pipeline replaced the temporary HTML workaround built for Parina.
Determine where the Osterkamp and McCarty alignment meetings stand, given Deepak's green light reported on 4/27.
Revisit the 'Architecture Recommendations' proposal Jonathan raised on 5/11 — decide whether it belongs on the current roadmap or remains an unfunded ask.
Establish what 'pressure testing' outcomes Jonathan Stewart and Mike Lester returned on the three-tier schema, and whether any structural changes resulted.
prep.title: Meeting with Jonathan

prep.summary: Your work with Deepak spans architecture governance, org navigation, and strategic tech decisions — the 5/6 meeting landed on formalizing architect roles in CR boards, automating dependency mapping via Bo's app-to-app tool, and a noted gap in SDLC execution-phase coverage. The EA SLT 5/6 session surfaced structural realignments (WDS/PI moving out of CTXO, Chad Cox's SDLC group shifting to Joe Monk), active SVP searches, mid-year talent calibrations running through mid-June, and a mandate to cut run budgets further for 2027. Open threads include the CMT/$14M POC bail-out clause and outcome metrics, the resiliency backlog of 160–170 apps stuck in pilot, and the stalled tech strategy publication pending the new CIO's review before the May board meeting.
tasks: Review the status of the architect CR board checklist and quick reference guide — confirm whether outreach to Chris, Michelle, and Deep about Oster Camp's integration has happened.
Check where Bo's automated app-to-app dependency map stands and whether the crowdsourcing initiative with architects on CRG calls has launched.
Confirm whether the 30-day bail-out clause and outcome metrics have been finalized in the CMT SOW.
Know your position on the SDLC execution-phase gap — be ready to discuss what closing that tollgate looks like in practice.
Review the talent calibration timeline and be prepared to discuss any AVP-level assessments Deepak expects from you before mid-June.
prep.title: Sync with Deepak
